edges.description: Loss of NHE1 proton extrusion directly impairs regulation of intracellular pH.
Dysregulated intracellular pH produces selective vulnerability and death of cerebellar neurons and cochlear hair cells.
Cerebellar neurodegeneration produces progressive cerebellar ataxia.
Cochlear hair cell vulnerability produces progressive sensorineural hearing loss.
Progressive cerebellar neuronal loss produces cerebellar atrophy on neuroimaging.
Cerebellar neurodegeneration can produce dysarthria as part of the progressive ataxia syndrome.
Cerebellar neurodegeneration can produce nystagmus as an oculomotor sign of the ataxia syndrome.
Slc9a1/NHE1 loss can produce recurrent seizures in the NHE1-loss phenotype spectrum.

nodes.description: Progressive cerebellar ataxia is a defining feature, presenting in childhood or young adulthood with gait ataxia, action tremor, dysmetria, and dysdiadochokinesis.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of SLC9A1, encoding the major Na⁺/H⁺ exchanger, causes ataxia-deafness Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome is an autosomal recessive condition that associates sensorineural hearing loss and cerebellar ataxia.

evidence.reference_title: A novel SLC9A1 mutation causes cerebellar ataxia.
evidence.snippet: The patients showed cerebellar ataxia but neither of them showed sensorineural hearing loss nor a neuromuscular phenotype.

nodes.description: Neuroimaging characteristically shows cerebellar atrophy consistent with the progressive cerebellar neurodegeneration.

evidence.reference_title: A novel SLC9A1 mutation causes cerebellar ataxia.
evidence.snippet: Mice with a homozygous null mutation in Slc9a1 (Nhe1) exhibited ataxia, recurrent seizures, and selective neuronal cell death.

nodes.description: Cochlear implantation is an option for severe-to-profound sensorineural hearing loss not adequately addressed by hearing aids.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of SLC9A1, encoding the major Na⁺/H⁺ exchanger, causes ataxia-deafness Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome is an autosomal recessive condition that associates sensorineural hearing loss and cerebellar ataxia.

nodes.description: Cerebellar dysarthria accompanies the progressive cerebellar syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of SLC9A1, encoding the major Na⁺/H⁺ exchanger, causes ataxia-deafness Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome is an autosomal recessive condition that associates sensorineural hearing loss and cerebellar ataxia.

nodes.description: Hearing aids and, for severe-to-profound sensorineural hearing loss, cochlear implantation are used to address the progressive deafness component.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of SLC9A1, encoding the major Na⁺/H⁺ exchanger, causes ataxia-deafness Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome is an autosomal recessive condition that associates sensorineural hearing loss and cerebellar ataxia.

nodes.description: NHE1 maintains intracellular pH by exchanging one intracellular proton for one extracellular sodium ion. Loss of NHE1 function leads to dysregulated intracellular pH, which is especially deleterious in the central nervous system.

evidence.reference_title: A novel SLC9A1 mutation causes cerebellar ataxia.
evidence.snippet: NHE1 plays an important role in maintaining intracellular pH homeostasis by exchanging one intracellular proton for one extracellular sodium ion.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of SLC9A1, encoding the major Na⁺/H⁺ exchanger, causes ataxia-deafness Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome.
evidence.snippet: the association between ataxia and hearing loss is caused by complete or near complete loss of function of NHE1 and altered regulation of pHi in the central nervous system

nodes.description: Nystagmus is an oculomotor cerebellar sign reported in the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of SLC9A1, encoding the major Na⁺/H⁺ exchanger, causes ataxia-deafness Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome is an autosomal recessive condition that associates sensorineural hearing loss and cerebellar ataxia.

nodes.description: Biallelic SLC9A1 variants cause loss of function of NHE1, the major plasma-membrane sodium/hydrogen exchanger. In the founding consanguineous family the homozygous p.Gly305Arg (c.913G>A) missense variant, affecting a highly conserved residue in the eighth transmembrane segment, causes near-complete de-glycosylation, mistargeting, and loss of proton-pumping activity of NHE1; truncating variants such as p.Ile288Serfs*9 also cause disease.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of SLC9A1, encoding the major Na⁺/H⁺ exchanger, causes ataxia-deafness Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome.
evidence.snippet: we identified the homozygous p.Gly305Arg missense mutation in SLC9A1 that segregates with the disease in a large consanguineous family

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of SLC9A1, encoding the major Na⁺/H⁺ exchanger, causes ataxia-deafness Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We demonstrate that the p.Gly305Arg mutation causes the near complete de-glycosylation, mis-targeting and loss of proton pumping activity of NHE1.

evidence.reference_title: A novel SLC9A1 mutation causes cerebellar ataxia.
evidence.snippet: we identified a novel homozygous SLC9A1 truncating mutation, c.862del (p.Ile288Serfs*9), in two affected siblings

evidence.reference_title: A novel SLC9A1 mutation causes cerebellar ataxia.
evidence.snippet: c.913G>A (p.Gly305Arg), which caused Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome characterized by cerebellar ataxia and sensorineural hearing loss

nodes.description: Seizures are part of the Slc9a1-null (slow-wave epilepsy, "swe") mouse phenotype and are reported in the broader SLC9A1-related disease spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: A novel SLC9A1 mutation causes cerebellar ataxia.
evidence.snippet: Mice with a homozygous null mutation in Slc9a1 (Nhe1) exhibited ataxia, recurrent seizures, and selective neuronal cell death.

nodes.description: Loss of NHE1-dependent pH regulation produces selective neuronal cell death. The cerebellum (including Purkinje cells) and the cochlea (sensory hair cells) are particularly vulnerable, producing progressive cerebellar ataxia and sensorineural hearing loss. The spontaneous and knockout Slc9a1 (Nhe1) mouse models recapitulate ataxia, recurrent seizures, and selective neuronal cell death.

evidence.reference_title: A novel SLC9A1 mutation causes cerebellar ataxia.
evidence.snippet: Mice with a homozygous null mutation in Slc9a1 (Nhe1) exhibited ataxia, recurrent seizures, and selective neuronal cell death.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of SLC9A1, encoding the major Na⁺/H⁺ exchanger, causes ataxia-deafness Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The comparison of our family with the phenotypes of spontaneous and knockout Slc9a1 murine models

nodes.description: Severe progressive sensorineural hearing loss is a defining feature in the founding family, although it may be absent in some patients with SLC9A1 variants.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of SLC9A1, encoding the major Na⁺/H⁺ exchanger, causes ataxia-deafness Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome is an autosomal recessive condition that associates sensorineural hearing loss and cerebellar ataxia.

evidence.reference_title: A novel SLC9A1 mutation causes cerebellar ataxia.
evidence.snippet: deafness may not be an essential phenotypic feature of SLC9A1 mutation

nodes.description: No disease-modifying therapy exists. Management is supportive, including physiotherapy and occupational therapy for ataxia, mobility aids, and seizure control where present.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of SLC9A1, encoding the major Na⁺/H⁺ exchanger, causes ataxia-deafness Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome is an autosomal recessive condition that associates sensorineural hearing loss and cerebellar ataxia.

nodes.description: Homozygous missense variant in the founding consanguineous family, affecting a highly conserved residue in the eighth transmembrane segment of NHE1; causes near-complete de-glycosylation, mistargeting, and loss of proton-pumping activity.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of SLC9A1, encoding the major Na⁺/H⁺ exchanger, causes ataxia-deafness Lichtenstein-Knorr syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Mutant glycine 305 is a highly conserved amino acid present in the eighth transmembrane segment of all metazoan orthologues of NHE1, the Na(+)/H(+) exchanger 1, encoded by SLC9A1.

nodes.description: Homozygous truncating frameshift variant identified in two affected siblings with cerebellar ataxia without hearing loss, expanding the SLC9A1 mutational and phenotypic spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: A novel SLC9A1 mutation causes cerebellar ataxia.
evidence.snippet: we identified a novel homozygous SLC9A1 truncating mutation, c.862del (p.Ile288Serfs*9), in two affected siblings
